title: -Contract: California Association of Environmental Health Administrators for Stormwater Inspections

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or designee to execute a contract and
any amendments with the California Association of Environmental Health Administrators
(CAEHA) to conduct facility stormwater inspections and other environmental health-
related inspections including food facility inspections, in collaboration with the City’s
Environmental Health Division (EHD) in an amount not to exceed $100,000 for the
period of February 1, 2026, through June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The Health, Housing, and Community Services Department’s (HHCS) EHD works
collaboratively with the Public Works Department to implement the stormwater
inspection program. This local program prohibits illicit discharges into Berkeley’s
stormwater drains. HHCS receives an annual allocation of approximately $500,000 to
inspect stormwater drains located at or near permitted food facilities. Funding is
available in the FY2026 Adopted Budget in the Sanitary Sewer Fund 611 and Public
Health Realignment Fund 156.
EHD filed a Request for Waiver of Competitive Solicitation (Waiver) for this contract
due to the need for staff who possess a highly-specialized Registered Environmental
Health Specialist credential. CAEHA is the only comprehensive resource and
clearinghouse for credentialed professionals who are qualified to perform facility
inspections. A similar contract with CAEHA was executed in 2025, which also included
a Waiver.
sections.current_situation:
The EHD is responsible for permitting and inspecting approximately 1,200 permitted
facilities city-wide. Restaurant facilities also require stormwater inspections to ensure
that pollutants are kept out of storm drains and that these facilities are in compliance
with state and local regulations.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 3
Contract: CA Association of Environmental Health Administrators CONSENT CALENDAR
for Stormwater Inspections February 24, 2026
Due to attrition and current staffing vacancies, the EHD does not have sufficient staffing
capacity to complete the number of stormwater inspections required by this state
program. EHD previously contracted with CAEHA to assist with stormwater inspections
in FY2025; CAEHA is in a position to provide similar assistance to EHD for FY2026.
sections.background:
The EHD administers various public health programs. The Restaurant Stormwater
Protection Inspection Program is a local program, required by Berkeley Municipal Code
(BMC) Title 17, Chapter 20, to prevent non-stormwater and pollutants from entering
drains. This program is also regulated by the State Water Resources Control Board.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Failure to inspect stormwater drains and regulate food facilities could potentially lead to
harmful environmental impacts, if pollutants are allowed to drain into community
waterways.
sections.rationale:
The recommended action provides the City with staffing support to conduct and
complete the required stormwater inspections for food facilities, as required by state and
local regulations.
sections.alternatives:
Council could consider not approving the contract; however, the health and safety of the
Berkeley community would be put at risk without adequately inspecting stormwater
drains located at or near food facilities.
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Gilman, Department Director, HHCS, (510) 981-5100
Ronald Torres, Manager, Environmental Health Division, HHCS, (510) 981-5261
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: CALIFORNIA ASSOCIATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH
ADMINISTRATORS FOR STORMWATER INSPECTIONS
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley’s Health, Housing & Community Services (HHCS)
Department is responsible for conducting stormwater inspections at or near permitted
food facilities; and
WHEREAS, HHCS seeks to abide by BMC 17.20 to prevent pollutants from entering the
City’s stormwater drains; and
WHEREAS, the California Association of Environmental Health Administrators was
contracted in FY2025 to assist in conducting stormwater inspections; and
WHEREAS, Funding is available in the FY2026 adopted budget in the Sanitary Sewer
Fund 611 and Public Health Realignment Fund 156,
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or designee is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with
the California Association of Environmental Health Administrators for required stormwater
inspections in an amount not to exceed $100,000 for the period of February 1, 2026,
through June 30, 2026.
